title: Schedule id truncated to first 8 chars of UUIDv4 — collision risk at 50+ schedules
description:
## Severity
Minor

## Dimension
Functional Correctness / KISS

## Location
src/tools/cron.rs:178 (Schedule.id assignment)

## Violation
```rust
id: Uuid::new_v4().to_string()[..8].to_string(),
```

UUID v4 has 122 bits of randomness; truncating to 8 hex chars discards 90 of those bits, leaving 32 bits → birthday collision probability of 50% at ~77k schedules and ~1% at ~9k schedules. Worse, schedules are stored in a Vec and looked up by `id != id_or_name && name != id_or_name` (line 218) — a collision between an existing id and a newly truncated UUID is silently undetectable, and `cron_delete` would delete the wrong schedule. The original line is fragile (`[..8]` panics if the UUID string is shorter than 8 chars — it won't, but the invariant is implicit).

## Mandated refactor
Store the full UUID string. The id is opaque — there is no UX cost to using the 36-char canonical form. Display only the first 8 chars in cron_list output (where they currently appear) but store and key by the full id. Also add a uniqueness assertion in cron_create: `assert!(store.schedules.iter().all(|s| s.id != schedule.id))` to fail-fast on the (now astronomically rare) collision.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
